all.en: When Marcus learned he would mentor the architecture firm's summer intern, he felt mildly nervous.
all.zh: 當 Marcus 得知他將要指導建築事務所的暑期實習生時，他感到有點緊張。

all.en: He had only been a senior designer for ten months himself.
all.zh: 他自己也才當上資深設計師十個月而已。

all.en: His boss reassured him, saying mentoring was the fastest way to grow professionally.
all.zh: 他的老闆向他保證，指導他人是專業成長最快的方法。

all.en: On Monday morning Marcus prepared a welcome folder with maps, passwords, and reading material.
all.zh: 週一早上 Marcus 準備了一個歡迎資料夾，裡面有地圖、密碼以及閱讀資料。

all.en: The intern, a quiet woman named Sophie, arrived ten minutes early with a polite handshake.
all.zh: 實習生 Sophie 是一位安靜的女生，提早十分鐘抵達，並有禮貌地與他握手。

all.en: She had just finished her third year of architecture school in Tainan.
all.zh: 她剛在台南念完建築系大三。

all.en: Marcus gave her a tour, introduced the team, and showed her the model-making workshop.
all.zh: Marcus 帶她參觀環境、介紹團隊，並讓她看了模型製作工作室。

all.en: Sophie's eyes lit up when she saw the rows of foam, cardboard, and small wooden blocks.
all.zh: 當 Sophie 看到一排排的泡棉板、紙板與小木塊時，她的眼睛亮了起來。

all.en: Her first assignment was to research sustainable materials for a community library project.
all.zh: 她的第一項任務是為一個社區圖書館專案研究永續建材。

all.en: Marcus told her she could ask any question, even ones that felt silly.
all.zh: Marcus 告訴她任何問題都可以問，即使是那些聽起來很傻的問題。

all.en: By Wednesday, Sophie returned with a thick binder of bamboo, cork, and recycled brick options.
all.zh: 到了週三，Sophie 帶著一本厚厚的活頁夾回來，裡面是竹子、軟木與回收磚的選項。

all.en: Marcus was impressed, though he gently suggested she focus on three favorites instead of fifteen.
all.zh: Marcus 印象深刻，不過他溫和地建議她聚焦在三個最喜歡的選項上，而不是十五個。

all.en: Sophie blushed but nodded, jotting down his advice in a neat little notebook.
all.zh: Sophie 紅著臉點頭，把他的建議寫進一本整齊的小筆記本裡。

all.en: Over the following weeks, they fell into a comfortable working rhythm.
all.zh: 在接下來的幾週裡，他們進入了一種舒服的工作節奏。

all.en: Each morning Sophie shared her sketches, and Marcus offered short, specific suggestions.
all.zh: 每天早晨 Sophie 都會分享她的草圖，而 Marcus 則提供簡短而具體的建議。

all.en: He tried hard never to redraw her ideas, only to ask questions that pointed her forward.
all.zh: 他非常努力地不去重畫她的構想，只用能引導她前進的問題來提問。

all.en: Halfway through the internship, Sophie made her first serious mistake in a measurement.
all.zh: 實習進行到一半時，Sophie 在一項丈量數據上犯了第一個嚴重的錯誤。

all.en: A whole study model had to be rebuilt over a long Friday evening.
all.zh: 整個研究模型必須在一個漫長的週五傍晚重新建造。

all.en: She apologized so many times that Marcus finally stopped her with a kind smile.
all.zh: 她道歉了太多次，Marcus 終於用一個和善的微笑制止了她。

all.en: He confessed that during his own internship he had once printed plans at the wrong scale.
all.zh: 他坦承在自己實習的時候，曾經把圖面以錯誤的比例印出來過。

all.en: Together they stayed until midnight rebuilding the model and listening to soft jazz.
all.zh: 他們一起待到午夜重建模型，並聽著輕柔的爵士樂。

all.en: Sophie said it was the first time she did not feel ashamed of a failure.
all.zh: Sophie 說那是她第一次不為自己的失敗感到羞愧。

all.en: In August, the firm held an internal review where each designer presented current work.
all.zh: 八月時，事務所舉辦了一場內部評圖，每位設計師都要介紹目前的作品。

all.en: Marcus invited Sophie to co-present the community library section with him.
all.zh: Marcus 邀請 Sophie 與他一起介紹社區圖書館的部分。

all.en: She spoke clearly about the bamboo facade idea she had championed since the start.
all.zh: 她清楚地說明了她從一開始就力推的竹製外牆構想。

all.en: Two senior partners praised her courage and the maturity of her thinking.
all.zh: 兩位資深合夥人讚賞她的勇氣以及她思考的成熟度。

all.en: After the meeting, the lead partner pulled Marcus aside in the hallway.
all.zh: 會議結束後，主導合夥人在走廊把 Marcus 叫到一旁。

all.en: He thanked Marcus for the way he had been guiding the intern with patience and respect.
all.zh: 他感謝 Marcus 一直以來用耐心與尊重的方式引導實習生。

all.en: Marcus realized that being a mentor had also taught him how to listen more deeply.
all.zh: Marcus 意識到當一名指導者也教會了他如何更深入地傾聽。

all.en: On Sophie's last day, the team gathered in the kitchen for cake and small speeches.
all.zh: 在 Sophie 的最後一天，團隊聚集在茶水間享用蛋糕並發表簡短的致詞。

all.en: She gave Marcus a hand-drawn card showing their library project framed by tall bamboo.
all.zh: 她送給 Marcus 一張手繪卡片，上面畫著他們的圖書館專案被高大的竹子圍繞著。

all.en: He kept it on his desk for years, long after she became a licensed architect.
all.zh: 他把卡片放在桌上好多年，直到她早已成為持照建築師之後依然如此。

all.en: Sophie returned to school more confident and started winning small design competitions.
all.zh: Sophie 帶著更高的自信回到學校，並開始在小型設計競賽中得獎。

all.en: Marcus, meanwhile, signed up to mentor another intern the very next summer.
all.zh: 與此同時，Marcus 也報名了在隔年夏天再指導另一位實習生。

all.en: He understood now that teaching someone else was the surest way to learn yourself.
all.zh: 他現在明白了，教導別人才是讓自己學習最確切的方式。

all.en: The new intern was a talkative young man named Roy from a coastal town in Yilan.
all.zh: 新的實習生是一位話多的年輕男生，名叫 Roy，來自宜蘭的一個海邊小鎮。

all.en: Roy loved discussing typhoon-resistant design and brought thick notebooks of childhood storm memories.
all.zh: Roy 喜歡討論抗颱風的設計，並帶來厚厚幾本兒時暴風雨記憶的筆記。

all.en: Marcus listened carefully and saw a fresh project angle no senior designer had considered.
all.zh: Marcus 仔細聆聽，看出了一個沒有資深設計師考慮過的全新專案切入點。

all.en: He proposed that Roy lead a small research piece on resilient coastal housing prototypes.
all.zh: 他提議讓 Roy 主導一個關於耐災沿海住宅原型的小型研究案。

all.en: Roy was startled by the responsibility but accepted with a wide grateful smile.
all.zh: Roy 對這份責任感到吃驚，但仍帶著燦爛而感激的微笑接受了。

all.en: Over the summer Roy interviewed fishermen, builders, and his own grandmother by long-distance phone.
all.zh: 整個夏天，Roy 用長途電話訪問了漁夫、建築工人，以及他自己的外婆。

all.en: He turned the stories into a slim booklet that the firm later printed for clients.
all.zh: 他把這些故事整理成一本薄薄的小冊子，事務所後來印製給客戶使用。

all.en: Marcus felt a strange pride that resembled what a teacher must feel at graduation.
all.zh: Marcus 感到一種奇妙的驕傲，類似老師在畢業典禮上會有的感受。

all.en: On the last day, Roy promised to send postcards from every site he would ever visit.
all.zh: 在最後一天，Roy 承諾從他將來會造訪的每一個工地都寄明信片給他。

all.en: Marcus pinned Sophie's drawing and Roy's first postcard side by side above his desk.
all.zh: Marcus 把 Sophie 的畫作與 Roy 寄來的第一張明信片並排釘在他的桌子上方。

all.en: Roy returned to school in autumn carrying fresh confidence and a folder of small project ideas.
all.zh: Roy 在秋天回到學校，帶著嶄新的自信與一個裝著小型專案構想的資料夾。

all.en: He shared his coastal housing booklet during a class review, surprising both his classmates and professors.
all.zh: 他在課堂評圖時分享了那本沿海住宅小冊子，讓同學與教授都感到驚訝。

all.en: One professor invited him to assist with a real research grant on typhoon-resistant building methods.
all.zh: 一位教授邀請他協助一項真正關於抗颱建築工法的研究計畫。

all.en: Sophie graduated and passed her licensing exams after three intense seasons of revision and practice.
all.zh: Sophie 在三個密集修讀與實作的學期後畢業，並通過了執照考試。

all.en: She accepted a position at a sustainable design studio just two blocks from Marcus and Roy.
all.zh: 她接下了一份在永續設計工作室的職位，距離 Marcus 與 Roy 只有兩條街。

all.en: The three friends began meeting monthly for coffee to discuss projects, struggles, and small triumphs.
all.zh: 三位朋友開始每月一次相約喝咖啡，討論專案、困境與小小的勝利。

all.en: Marcus quietly realized that mentoring had built a network richer than any conference could provide.
all.zh: Marcus 悄悄體會到，指導他人所建立的人脈比任何研討會都更為豐厚。

all.en: His lead partner asked him to design a formal mentorship program for new interns across the firm.
all.zh: 他的主導合夥人請他為事務所所有新進實習生設計一個正式的指導計畫。

all.en: Marcus accepted, drafting weekly check-ins, reading lists, and gentle feedback routines for future pairs.
all.zh: Marcus 答應了，為未來的師徒配對草擬了每週進度會議、閱讀書單與溫和的回饋流程。

all.en: By year end, six interns had completed the program with stronger portfolios and clearer career goals.
all.zh: 到了年底，已有六位實習生完成這個計畫，作品集更加紮實，職涯方向也更為明確。

all.en: Last Saturday morning, Marcus met Sophie and Roy at their usual cafe for their monthly catch-up.
all.zh: 上週六早上，Marcus 與 Sophie、Roy 在他們慣常的咖啡館碰面，進行每月一次的聚會。

all.en: Sophie arrived first with a folder of her studio's latest sustainable housing renderings.
all.zh: Sophie 第一個到，帶著一份她工作室最新永續住宅渲染圖的資料夾。

all.en: Roy came in carrying three iced lattes and a paper bag full of warm cheese tarts.
all.zh: Roy 接著進來，手上拿著三杯冰拿鐵和一個裝滿熱起司塔的紙袋。

all.en: Marcus laughed and said Roy had clearly inherited his coastal grandmother's hospitality instincts.
all.zh: Marcus 笑說 Roy 顯然遺傳了他海邊外婆熱情待客的天性。

all.en: Sophie shared a tricky permit problem her studio had been wrestling with all week.
all.zh: Sophie 分享了她工作室整週都在處理的一個棘手建照問題。

all.en: Marcus suggested she call a contact at the city planning office whom he had met two years ago.
all.zh: Marcus 建議她聯絡他兩年前在都市計畫處認識的一位窗口。

all.en: Roy admitted he was nervous about his upcoming first solo client meeting next Tuesday afternoon.
all.zh: Roy 坦承他下週二下午第一次獨自跟客戶開會，心裡很緊張。

all.en: Sophie offered to role-play the client with him over the weekend at her tiny studio kitchen.
all.zh: Sophie 主動表示週末可以在她小小的工作室廚房裡，跟他角色扮演客戶演練一下。

all.en: Marcus joked that he was now the oldest member of an unofficial mentorship triangle.
all.zh: Marcus 開玩笑說自己現在成了這個非正式師徒三角中最資深的成員。

all.en: They paid the bill together, each insisting it was their turn this time around.
all.zh: 他們一起結帳，每個人都堅持這次該換自己請。

all.en: Walking out into the warm afternoon sunlight, Marcus felt that mentoring had truly become a shared circle.
all.zh: 走出咖啡館迎著溫暖午後陽光，Marcus 感到指導已真正成為一個共享的圓圈。
